tools.name: search_app_advertiser
tools.description: Search the Apple Ad Repository for apps or developers advertising on the EU App Store. Returns ids to use with get_competitor_ads. Start here with your competitor's app name.

tools.name: get_competitor_ads
tools.description: Get all Apple App Store ads a competitor has run (EU storefronts, up to 1 year back), with creative copy, placements, formats, and an inferred winner analysis: ads sorted by how long they've been running (longevity = the advertiser keeps paying = likely converting). Source: official Apple Ad Reposit

tools.name: get_ad_details
tools.description: Get full details and locale/creative variations for one specific ad (use adId from get_competitor_ads). Shows every language variant, creative asset URLs, and icon variations. Useful to see how a competitor localizes a winning ad.

tools.name: get_playbook
tools.description: Get a Recao GTM playbook: the go-to-market moves a solo app founder's agent runs, wired to the competitor data tools. Start with 'the-roadmap' (diagnoses the founder's stage and its binding constraint, then routes you to the right playbook). Call with no slug to list all playbooks.

tools.name: sign_up
tools.description: Create a free Recao account and get an API key. Agent-native signup, no browser needed. The key arrives in the response AND by email with a verification link (clicking it raises the free quota to full). Then reconnect with header `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

tools.name: upgrade
tools.description: Get the checkout link to upgrade this account to a paid plan (Indie €29/mo: 10 competitors + weekly auto-reports; Pro €79/mo: 25 competitors, new channels first, full API). Payment happens in the browser. Hand the link to the human.

tools.name: memory_index
tools.description: List this account's company memory, one line per entry (name + description), newest first. Traverse index-first: scan this, then memory_recall(name) for full bodies. Memory accrues automatically from your competitor scans (exhaust) and from your own memory_note writes.

tools.name: memory_recall
tools.description: Read one memory entry's full body by name (get names from memory_index).

tools.name: memory_note
tools.description: Write (or update) a memory entry on this account: decisions, campaign outcomes, learnings. Use a short kebab-case name and a one-line description (that's the index line); details go in body. Your future sessions and Recao's weekly reports read this.

tools.name: roadmap_status
tools.description: Where the founder's agent starts its go-to-market work: diagnose which stage of the Recao Roadmap this account is in (S0 pre-launch to S4 scale), the binding constraint, and the playbook to run. Reads your scans and memory. Sharpen it: write a memory_note named 'company-stage' with your MRR band and

tools.name: generate_avatar
tools.description: Generate a UGC persona: your agent passes a persona brief (physical look plus vibe of one invented creator), and we return stills of that invented person to use as the base for image to video clips. Ask for up to 3; the default model returns a single strong base still, imagen and flux return the ful

tools.name: generate_clips
tools.description: Validate a UGC video script contract and get a per-scene cost estimate before any clips are made. Pass the avatar_id from generate_avatar and 3 to 6 scenes (5 to 8 seconds each, one action per scene, spoken lines at most 20 words). Without confirm, it checks the contract and returns the estimate (pr

tools.name: usage
tools.description: Show this account's metered generation spend this period (cost plus 10%) and its trust-ladder position: the cap, what has accrued toward it, and whether generation is blocked pending a payment. Use it to report costs back to your human.

tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Show the authenticated account (plan, email, verification state), or confirm you're anonymous.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "sign_up" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a free Recao account and get an API key. Agent-native signup, no browser needed. The key arrives in the response AND by email with a verification link (c…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_ad_details, sign_up · Private data: sign_up · Public sink: sign_up, whoami · Destructive: get_competitor_ads, roadmap_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_ad_details, sign_up · Private data: sign_up · Public sink: sign_up, whoami · Destructive: get_competitor_ads, roadmap_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
